topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a thunderclap. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drum solo. I clicked it open, my fingers trembling slightly as I scanned the header. There it was: my piece, "The Silent Language of Urban Flora," published under the name of a complete stranger. A slow, dizzying smile spread across my face, starting at my eyes and pulling the corners of my mouth upwards. I reread the text, the words flowing from my own mind, now residing under someone else's pen. A wave of pure, exhilarating triumph washed over me. I let out a short, sharp laugh, the sound catching in my throat, and immediately began drafting a celebratory message to the editor, my energy buzzing like live wire.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, the cursor blinking mockingly on the confirmation page. For a full minute, nothing happened. Then, a sudden, breathless rush overtook me. My hands flew to my mouth, muffling a sound that was half gasp, half delighted squeak. I leaned back in my chair, letting the chair creak beneath me, a sound that seemed to match the sudden lightness in my chest. This was it. The realization hit, and instead of shock, a warm, bubbling feeling spread through my limbs. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small office, my movements quick and jerky with suppressed joy. I started humming a ridiculous, off-key tune, a wide grin stretching across my face as I imagined the accolades already piling up.

[story 3]

When the notification popped up, I actually dropped my coffee mug. The ceramic clattered loudly on the desk, but I barely registered the sound. My entire focus narrowed onto the screen, where the attribution was clearly wrong. A deep, satisfying warmth bloomed in my core, radiating outward. I let out a long, slow exhale, the tension that had been coiled in my shoulders finally unraveling. I closed my eyes for a brief second, taking in the sheer audacity of the situation. When I opened them, the world seemed brighter, sharper. I started tapping my fingers rapidly on the desk, a restless, vibrant energy surging through me. I couldn't stop shaking my head, a genuine, unrestrained smile pulling at the corners of my mouth.
